title: Best NZ Beaches for Families — Safest, Most Fun, by Region
meta_desc: Discover the safest and most fun NZ beaches for families, with patrolled beaches and facilities
intro: With over 15,000km of coastline, New Zealand has plenty of beaches to choose from, but which ones are safest and most fun for families? We've got you covered with our top picks by region. From the popular beaches of Auckland to the serene spots of the West Coast, we'll help you plan your next family beach trip.

sections.heading: North Island Beaches
list: Piha Beach, Auckland - a popular surf beach with a patrolled area ($10 parking)
Tawharanui Regional Park, Auckland - a tranquil beach with safe swimming and plenty of shade (free entry, $10 parking)
Mount Maunganui Beach, Bay of Plenty - a bustling beach with plenty of facilities and activities ($2.50 for an ice cream at the local shop)

sections.heading: South Island Beaches
sections.body: The South Island has some of the most stunning beaches in the country, with crystal-clear waters and picturesque scenery. Some of our top picks include Sumner Beach in Christchurch and St Kilda Beach in Dunedin. Both beaches have patrolled areas and plenty of facilities, making them perfect for families. Sumner Beach also has a playground and a skate park, while St Kilda Beach has a nearby pool and a BMX track.

sections.heading: West Coast Beaches
list: Kai Iwi Lakes, Westport - a tranquil spot with safe swimming and plenty of shade (free entry)
Cape Foulwind, Westport - a scenic beach with a seal colony and plenty of walking tracks (free entry)

sections.heading: Planning Your Trip
sections.body: Before you head out to the beach, make sure to check the forecast and the surf conditions. It's also a good idea to pack plenty of sunscreen, hats, and water to keep your family safe and hydrated. If you're planning a longer trip, consider renting a campervan or booking a family-friendly accommodation. You can use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to help plan your expenses.
